summary: watch check treats a non-positive scraped price as 'no price data', never a drop: such items get hit=false, land in a top-level no_price[] bucket (reason no_price_data), and never trigger the exit-7 drop sentinel. Exit 7 fires only when a real positive price is at or below a positive max-price. The decision is the pure helper classifyWatchPrice(price, maxPrice) -> (hasPrice, hit).
reason: firstPlatformTotals returns 0 both for a genuinely-free listing and for one whose SSR price was unavailable for the dates. The old `price <= MaxPrice` was always true for price==0 against any positive threshold, so an unavailable price produced a false hit and a spurious exit 7 (the watch drop signal) on every cron run. Exit 7 is dual-purpose (HTTP 429 AND the watch drop-hit success signal), so the guard must live in watch check where the watchlist intent is known. A regen must keep classifyWatchPrice's price>0 guard and the {hits,no_price} top-level object envelope (bare arrays lose non-allowlisted fields under --agent/--compact).
validated_outcome: go build/vet green; classifyWatchPrice table test: price 0/negative => no hit for any threshold; positive price hits only at or under a positive threshold.
